A1.guidance: Prefer matching and categorization tasks. For fill-in-blank items, always provide a word bank — list the answer options in the hint field. Max 3 options for multiple-choice. Do not include sentence transformation or error correction tasks.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Sentence transformation requires prior command of the target form; inappropriate at A1
Error correction requires meta-awareness of the rule; too demanding at A1
Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Reading comprehension tasks are not practice exercises for grammar/vocabulary at A1
Extended writing is production, not controlled practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Always provide a word bank listing all answer options in the hint field
Maximum 3 options per item at A1
Use only 3-4 words per sentence to order; longer sequences are confusing at A1

A2.guidance: Fill-in-blank with optional word bank, multiple-choice with 4 options, true/false with justification required. Use simple contextual items rather than isolated drills.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Sentence transformation requires prior command of the target form; not yet appropriate at A2
Error correction requires meta-awareness of the rule; too demanding at A2
Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Extended writing is production, not controlled practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Maximum 4 options per item at A2
Word bank is optional at A2 but recommended for new structures

B1.guidance: Use at least 2 different exercise formats (e.g. fill-in-blank AND multiple-choice — do not rely on just one type). Include transformation and error correction items.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Extended writing is production, not controlled practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Sentence-level corrections only at B1; discourse-level error correction is B2+
Use a short (80-120 word) authentic-style source text from which student extracts and organises the target forms

B2.guidance: Pragmatic appropriateness, register selection, discourse completion tasks. Multi-step activities with nuanced distractors. Error correction at discourse level.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Extended writing is production, not controlled practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Discourse-level error correction is appropriate at B2; include cohesion and register errors, not only morphosyntax

C1.guidance: Minimize purely mechanical items (basic fill-in-blank, simple matching) in favor of reformulation, paraphrase, register transfer, and pragmatic inference tasks.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Basic fill-in-blank is too mechanical for C1; use reformulation or transformation tasks instead
Simple multiple choice is too mechanical for C1
Paradigm drilling is beneath C1 level
Simple matching is too mechanical for C1
Word ordering is too mechanical for C1
Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Ludic activities belong to warm-up or optional extension, not structured practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Register transformation at C1 should require precise control of register markers, not just lexical swaps
Critical reading in practice: student argues against the text's position to demonstrate analytical distance

C2.guidance: Reformulation, paraphrase, register transfer, pragmatic inference. No mechanical drills. Tasks require nuance and register awareness. Student evaluates alternative formulations.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Basic fill-in-blank is too mechanical for C2; use reformulation or transformation tasks instead
Simple multiple choice is too mechanical for C2
Paradigm drilling is beneath C2 level
Simple matching is too mechanical for C2
Word ordering is too mechanical for C2
Inductive discovery is a presentation task, not a practice drill
Ludic activities belong to warm-up or optional extension, not structured practice

levelSpecificNotes.note: Written mediation at C2: student rewrites a specialized text for a non-specialist audience, maintaining accuracy and adjusting register
Error correction at C2 includes pragmatic inappropriateness and register violations, not only grammatical errors
